word_list.word: a barber learns to shave by shaving fools
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ bˈɑːɹbɚ lˈɜːnz tə ʃˈeɪv baɪ ʃˈeɪvɪŋ fˈuːlz

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of practice and experience in developing expertise, and the value of learning from mistakes

examples.x: Don't criticize the new intern so harshly, remember the saying - a barber learns to shave by shaving fools.

word_list.word: bought wit is the best wit
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɔːt wˈɪt ɪz ðə bˈɛst wˈɪt

senses.definition: used to imply that knowledge or wisdom gained through education or experience is more valuable than innate intelligence or natural ability

examples.x: Although she wasn't naturally gifted at math, her hard work and dedication paid off when she aced her calculus final - proving that bought wit is the best wit.

word_list.word: the child is the father of the man
word_list.pronunciation: ðə tʃˈaɪld ɪz ðə fˈɑːðɚɹ ʌvðə mˈæn

senses.definition: used to imply that the experiences and influences of childhood shape a person's adult identity, emphasizing the importance of early childhood development in shaping a person's future

examples.x: Looking back on his own childhood, he realized that his love for nature and the outdoors was largely shaped by his experiences exploring the woods behind his childhood home - a perfect example of how the child is the father of the man.

word_list.word: (the man|he|one) who has once been bitten by a snake, fears every piece of rope
word_list.pronunciation: ðə mˈæn hiː wˈʌn hˌuː hɐz wˈʌns bˌɪn bˈɪʔn̩ baɪ ɐ snˈeɪk fˈɪɹz ˈɛvɹi pˈiːs ʌv ɹˈoʊp

senses.definition: used to suggest that past experiences can influence a person's future behavior and attitudes, and that negative experiences can lead to unnecessary fear or caution

word_list.word: there is always a first time
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz ɐ fˈɜːst tˈaɪm

senses.definition: used to emphasize the idea that everyone starts from a point of inexperience before gaining knowledge or mastery in a certain area

examples.x: She had never been skiing before, but took the plunge and booked a lesson, knowing that there is always a first time for everything.

word_list.word: to know the road ahead, ask those (who are|) coming back
word_list.pronunciation: tə nˈoʊ ðə ɹˈoʊd ɐhˈɛd ˈæsk ðoʊz hˌuː ɑːɹ kˈʌmɪŋ bˈæk

senses.definition: used to suggest that learning from the experiences of others can be valuable in navigating new or unfamiliar situations

word_list.word: travel broadens the mind
word_list.pronunciation: tɹˈævəl bɹˈɔːdənz ðə mˈaɪnd

senses.definition: used to suggest that exposure to diverse people, ideas, and experiences can enhance a person's knowledge, empathy, and overall worldview

examples.x: After spending a year backpacking through Europe, I can attest to the fact that travel broadens the mind.

word_list.word: years know (way|much) more than books (alone|)
word_list.pronunciation: jˈɪɹz nˈoʊ wˈeɪ mˈʌtʃ mˈoːɹ ðɐn bˈʊks ɐlˈoʊn

senses.definition: used to imply that experience gained over time and through life's challenges and lessons can be more valuable than knowledge gained solely from books or formal education

word_list.word: you cannot put an old head on young shoulders
word_list.pronunciation: juː kænˈɑːt pˌʊt ɐn ˈoʊld hˈɛd ˌɑːn jˈʌŋ ʃˈoʊldɚz

senses.definition: used to suggest that wisdom and maturity come with age and experience, and that they cannot be easily taught or forced onto someone who is young and inexperienced

word_list.word: an old dog barks not in vain
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈoʊld dˈɑːɡ bˈɑːɹks nˌɑːt ɪn vˈeɪn

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of valuing the knowledge and experience of older individuals, as they often have valuable insights and reasons for their actions

examples.x: Some thought the elderly woman's critiques of society were outdated and irrelevant, but an old dog barks not in vain, for experience brings valuable perspective.

word_list.word: an old dog for a hard road
word_list.pronunciation: ɐn ˈoʊld dˈɑːɡ fɚɹə hˈɑːɹd ɹˈoʊd

senses.definition: used to imply that an experienced individual is better suited to handle challenging or difficult situations than a younger or less experienced person

word_list.word: experience is (always|) the best teacher
word_list.pronunciation: ɛkspˈiəɹɪəns ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz ðə bˈɛst tˈiːtʃɚ

senses.definition: used to emphasize the value of learning through personal experience, which is often more effective than theoretical or academic knowledge

examples.x: Experience is the best teacher, so she studied her failures closely and used them to guide her future successes.

word_list.word: experience is the (father|mother) of wisdom
word_list.pronunciation: ɛkspˈiəɹɪəns ɪz ðə fˈɑːðɚ mˈʌðɚ ʌv wˈɪsdəm

senses.definition: used to imply that practical experience and learning from past mistakes is a valuable source of knowledge and understanding

examples.x: The seasoned politician's advice was invaluable, for experience is the father of wisdom and he had navigated many political waters over the decades.

word_list.word: experience is (deemed as|) the mistress of fools
word_list.pronunciation: ɛkspˈiəɹɪəns ɪz dˈiːmd æz ðə mˈɪstɹəs ʌv fˈuːlz

senses.definition: used to imply that foolish individuals tend to learn from their own mistakes rather than heeding the advice or experiences of others

word_list.word: experience is the teacher of fools
word_list.pronunciation: ɛkspˈiəɹɪəns ɪz ðə tˈiːtʃɚɹ ʌv fˈuːlz

senses.definition: used to imply that some people may be unwilling or unable to learn from the advice of others and may only learn important lessons through personal experience and making mistakes

word_list.word: tell me and I will forget, show me, and I may not remember, involve me and I will understand
word_list.pronunciation: tˈɛl mˌiː ænd aɪ wɪl fɚɡˈɛt ʃˈoʊ mˌiː ænd aɪ mˈeɪ nˌɑːt ɹɪmˈɛmbɚ ɪnvˈɑːlv mˌiː ænd aɪ wɪl ˌʌndɚstˈænd

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of active learning in contrast to simply being told or shown

word_list.word: only the wearer knows where the shoe pinches
word_list.pronunciation: ˈoʊnli ðə wˈɛɹɚ nˈoʊz wˌɛɹ ðə ʃˈuː pˈɪntʃᵻz

senses.definition: used to imply that only the person who is directly experiencing a particular problem or difficulty can fully understand the extent of their discomfort or pain

examples.x: Though people tried to help, they could never fully grasp the complexities of her circumstances, for only the wearer knows where the shoe pinches and where the most discomfort lies.

word_list.word: they that live (the|) longest see (the|) most
word_list.pronunciation: ðeɪ ðæt lˈaɪv ðə lˈɑːŋɡəst sˈiː ðə mˈoʊst

senses.definition: used to imply that the longer people live, the more experiences and knowledge they gather, which can lead to a deeper understanding of life and the world around them

examples.x: While the young are full of vigor and ambition, they that live longest see most and the older generations possess a depth of insight that only time can provide.

word_list.word: learn to say before you sing
word_list.pronunciation: lˈɜːn tə sˈeɪ bɪfˌoːɹ juː sˈɪŋ

senses.definition: used to imply that mastering the basics or fundamentals of a skill or subject is essential before attempting more advanced or complex aspects of it

examples.x: The chef started with the basics of cooking before attempting more complex recipes - learn to say before you sing.

word_list.word: learning is the eye of the mind
word_list.pronunciation: lˈɜːnɪŋ ɪz ðɪ ˈaɪ ʌvðə mˈaɪnd

senses.definition: used to imply that education and continuous learning are essential for gaining knowledge, understanding, and expanding one's mental horizons

examples.x: As a teacher, I try to instill in my students the idea that learning is the eye of the mind, and that by pursuing knowledge and education, they can gain a deeper understanding of the world around them.

word_list.word: experience without learning is (far|way|) better than learning without experience
word_list.pronunciation: ɛkspˈiəɹɪəns wɪðˌaʊt lˈɜːnɪŋ ɪz fˈɑːɹ wˈeɪ bˈɛɾɚ ðɐn lˈɜːnɪŋ wɪðˌaʊt ɛkspˈiəɹɪəns

senses.definition: used to imply that practical experience and hands-on learning are more significant and impactful than acquiring knowledge solely through theoretical or academic means

examples.x: He learned more about the business world through his own mistakes and failures than he ever did in the classroom, demonstrating how experience without learning is better than learning without experience.

word_list.word: when house and land are gone and spent, then learning is most excellent
word_list.pronunciation: wˌɛn hˈaʊs ænd lˈænd ɑːɹ ɡɔn ænd spˈɛnt ðˈɛn lˈɜːnɪŋ ɪz mˈoʊst ˈɛksələnt

senses.definition: used to imply that education and knowledge are the most important assets one can have, especially in difficult times when material possessions have been lost or no longer hold value

word_list.word: the eye of the master will do more work than both his hands
word_list.pronunciation: ðɪ ˈaɪ ʌvðə mˈæstɚ wɪl dˈuː mˈoːɹ wˈɜːk ðɐn bˈoʊθ hɪz hˈændz

senses.definition: used to imply that careful supervision and attention to detail by a skilled supervisor or master can enhance work efficiency and quality, even without physically participating in the work
